tests.name: InMemoryCacheStore — construction rejects non-positive or non-integer maxEntries (fail-closed)
InMemoryCacheStore — construction defaults the clock to Date.now when none is injected
InMemoryCacheStore — basic semantics get/has/delete on an absent key
InMemoryCacheStore — basic semantics stores and retrieves; delete returns true for a live entry then false
InMemoryCacheStore — basic semantics overwrites an existing key in place
InMemoryCacheStore — TTL expiry at the exact boundary rejects negative or NaN ttlMs (fail-closed)
InMemoryCacheStore — TTL expiry at the exact boundary is live at exactly expiresAt and dead one tick after
InMemoryCacheStore — TTL expiry at the exact boundary ttlMs of 0 means live only at the instant of insertion
InMemoryCacheStore — TTL expiry at the exact boundary delete reports false for an expired-but-present entry, and purges it
InMemoryCacheStore — TTL expiry at the exact boundary expired entries free capacity (lazy purge on access)
InMemoryCacheStore — TTL expiry at the exact boundary a no-TTL entry never expires regardless of clock advance
InMemoryCacheStore — LRU eviction at capacity evicts the least-recently-used entry when capacity is exceeded
InMemoryCacheStore — LRU eviction at capacity get() refreshes recency so the touched key survives eviction
InMemoryCacheStore — LRU eviction at capacity overwriting a key refreshes its recency
InMemoryCacheStore — LRU eviction at capacity never exceeds maxEntries under sustained inserts; keeps the most recent
InMemoryCacheStore — LRU eviction at capacity capacity of 1 keeps only the newest
ResultCache.getOrCompute — miss then hit computes on miss (cached:false) and serves from cache on hit (cached:true)
ResultCache.getOrCompute — miss then hit calls computeFn exactly once across repeated hits on the same key
ResultCache.getOrCompute — miss then hit supports async compute functions
ResultCache.getOrCompute — distinct keys are independent different requests each miss once and never share results
ResultCache.getOrCompute — distinct keys are independent param-order-equivalent requests hit the same cache entry
ResultCache.getOrCompute — tenant isolation (threat T1) the same logical request for two tenants never shares a cached value
ResultCache.getOrCompute — error and TTL behaviour does not cache a result when computeFn throws
ResultCache.getOrCompute — error and TTL behaviour applies the default TTL so entries expire and recompute
ResultCache.getOrCompute — error and TTL behaviour with no default TTL, entries persist indefinitely
hashImageBytes returns a 64-char lowercase hex digest
hashImageBytes is content-addressed: identical bytes hash identically, different bytes differ
hashImageBytes matches the known SHA-256 of an empty input
deriveCacheKey — shape produces <tenantId>:<sha256hex>
deriveCacheKey — determinism property: same input yields identical key across many runs
deriveCacheKey — tenant isolation (threat T1) property: keys differing only by tenantId are never equal
deriveCacheKey — tenant isolation (threat T1) resists boundary-shifting collisions across tenant/product split
deriveCacheKey — tenant isolation (threat T1) keeps adversarial tenantIds containing ":" and "%" in disjoint prefixes
deriveCacheKey — tenant isolation (threat T1) the digest itself (not just the prefix) differs by tenant
deriveCacheKey — param normalization is invariant to param key ordering
deriveCacheKey — param normalization property: reordering param keys never changes the key
deriveCacheKey — param normalization distinguishes different params, image hashes, and products
deriveCacheKey — param normalization propagates fail-closed errors from canonical serialization
canonicalJsonStringify — primitives serializes primitives exactly like JSON for finite scalars
canonicalJsonStringify — primitives distinguishes 0 from "0" from false (no type confusion)
canonicalJsonStringify — key ordering invariance produces identical output regardless of object key insertion order
canonicalJsonStringify — key ordering invariance property: shuffling object keys never changes the output
canonicalJsonStringify — key ordering invariance preserves array order (arrays are sequence-semantic, not sorted)
canonicalJsonStringify — fail-closed on unrepresentable values throws on non-finite numbers rather than coercing to null
canonicalJsonStringify — fail-closed on unrepresentable values throws on undefined members rather than silently dropping them
canonicalJsonStringify — fail-closed on unrepresentable values throws on top-level unsupported types
canonicalJsonStringify — nested determinism property property: output is parseable and round-trips to an equivalent value
deriveCacheKey — hashField length-prefix (mutation-hardening) boundary-shifted adjacent fields produce DIFFERENT digests, not just different prefixes
deriveCacheKey — hashField length-prefix (mutation-hardening) boundary shift between personImageHash and productId also diverges in the digest
deriveCacheKey — prefixSafe delimiter encoding (mutation-hardening) a tenant id containing ":" cannot forge another tenant's prefix namespace
deriveCacheKey — prefixSafe delimiter encoding (mutation-hardening) percent-encodes "%" so the escape itself cannot be spoofed
